atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. I'll do it later → 3. Accept terms → 4. Skip onboarding → 5. Tap camera icon (bottom-right) → 6. Verify camera screen
atpExpected: User should be able to open the app's camera from the gallery

yamlFileName: CA_02 - Open camera go back to gallery.yaml
testName: CA_02 - Open camera go back to gallery
atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. Tap camera icon located at the bottom-right of the screen → 3. Tap return icon located at the top-left of the screen
atpExpected: User should be able to open the app camera and go back to gallery

atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. Tap camera (bottom-right) → 3. Tap flash until slash icon → 4. Capture image
atpExpected: User should be able to capture images with flash disabled

atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. Tap camera (bottom-right) → 3. Tap flash until solid icon → 4. Capture image
atpExpected: User should be able to capture images with flash enabled

yamlFileName: CA_05 - Capture with flash set to auto.yaml
testName: CA_05 - Capture with flash set to auto
atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. Tap camera (bottom-right) → 3. Tap flash until auto (A) icon → 4. Capture image
atpExpected: User should be able to capture images with automatic flash

yamlFileName: CA_06 - Change to front facing camera.yaml
testName: CA_06 - Change to front facing camera
atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. Tap camera (bottom-right) → 3. Tap camera flip icon
atpExpected: User should be able to switch to the front facing camera of their mobile device

yamlFileName: CA_07 - Capture using front camera with no flash.yaml
testName: CA_07 - Capture using front camera with no flash
atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. Tap camera (bottom-right) → 3. Flip to front camera → 4. Tap flash until slash icon → 5. Capture image
atpExpected: User should be able to capture using the front facing camera. Flash should not engage

yamlFileName: CA_08 - Capture using front camera with flash enabled.yaml
testName: CA_08 - Capture using front camera with flash enabled
atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. Tap camera (bottom-right) → 3. Flip to front camera → 4. Tap flash until solid icon → 5. Capture image
atpExpected: User should be able to capture using the front facing camera with the front facing flash

yamlFileName: CA_09 - Capture using front camera with flash set to auto.yaml
testName: CA_09 - Capture using front camera with flash set to auto
atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. Tap camera (bottom-right) → 3. Flip to front camera → 4. Tap flash until auto (A) icon → 5. Capture image
atpExpected: User should be able to capture using the front facing camera with auto front facing flash

atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. Capture image → 3. Tap capture thumbnail (bottom-left)
atpExpected: User should be able to open the captured image

atpSteps: 1. Open app → 2. Tap camera (bottom-right) → 3. Verify camera opens (permissions auto-enabled on test devices)
atpExpected: Camera opens when permissions are already granted on test devices

atpSteps: 1. Open camera → 2–4. Capture three photos in succession → 5. Verify camera remains on capture screen
atpExpected: User should be able to capture multiple photos rapidly without leaving the camera

atpSteps: 1. Open camera → 2. Capture photo → 3. Verify low-storage error message
atpExpected: When device storage is full, capturing a photo shows an appropriate error
notes: Requires device near full storage; may be skipped in CI

atpSteps: 1. Open camera → 2–4. Switch front/back camera three times → 5. Verify flip control and capture button remain available
atpExpected: User should be able to switch between front and rear cameras repeatedly without failure
